Interface IExchangeKindType

All Known Implementing Classes:
GTNetExchangeKindType
public interface IExchangeKindType
Interface for exchange kind type enums. Allows message classes in the base module to work with exchange kinds without depending on specific implementations. Application modules can provide their own enum implementations with specific exchange kind values.

Usage Example

public enum GTNetExchangeKindType implements IExchangeKindType {
    LAST_PRICE((byte) 0),
    HISTORICAL_PRICES((byte) 1);
    // ...
}

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Byte
    getValue()
    Returns the byte value representing this exchange kind.
    default boolean
    isSyncable()
    Indicates whether this exchange kind participates in bulk synchronization.
    String
    name()
    Returns the enum constant name.
    default boolean
    supportsPush()
    Indicates whether this exchange kind supports AC_PUSH_OPEN mode.

  • Method Details

    • getValue

      Byte getValue()
      Returns the byte value representing this exchange kind.
      Returns:
      the byte value for serialization/deserialization

    • name

      String name()
      Returns the enum constant name.
      Returns:
      the name of this enum constant

    • isSyncable

      default boolean isSyncable()
      Indicates whether this exchange kind participates in bulk synchronization. Syncable kinds (like LAST_PRICE, HISTORICAL_PRICES) participate in data request/accept/revoke flows and are included in default exchange configurations. Non-syncable kinds (like SECURITY_METADATA) use on-demand lookup patterns and are excluded from bulk sync operations.
      Returns:
      true if this kind participates in bulk sync, false for on-demand lookup kinds

    • supportsPush

      default boolean supportsPush()
      Indicates whether this exchange kind supports AC_PUSH_OPEN mode. Push-enabled kinds can be configured with AC_PUSH_OPEN to actively receive pushed updates from remote instances. Kinds that don't support push (like SECURITY_METADATA) can only use AC_CLOSED or AC_OPEN modes.
      Returns:
      true if this kind supports AC_PUSH_OPEN configuration, false otherwise
